name: Desert de les Palmes Loop from Benicàssim
name_original: Parque Natural del Desert de Les Palmes — Circular desde Benicàssim
description: Tackle the difficult 20.4-mile Desert de les Palmes mountain bike loop from Benicàssim, featuring 3402 feet of climbing and stunning coastal
region_intro: Mountain biking around Benicàssim / Benicasim offers diverse terrain, situated between the Mediterranean Sea and the mountains of the Desierto de las Palmas. The region features a mix of coastal paths, such as the Vía Verde del Mar, and challenging mountainous routes within the Desierto de las Palmas Natural Park. This area is part of Castellón, one of Spain's most mountainous provinces, providing varied elevation and landscapes for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Benicàssim / Benicasim The most popular mountain bike trail is Desert de les Palmes Loop from Benicàssim, a difficult 20.4 miles (32.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 30 minutes to complete. This route explores the mountainous terrain of the Desierto de las Palmas. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Greenway of the Sea – Marina d'Or Garden loop from La Curva, a moderate 24.8 miles (39.9 km) path. This trail offers continuous sea views along the Vía Verde del Mar. Local mountain bikers also love the Greenway of the Sea – View of La Plana de Cabanes loop from Benicàssim, a 49.3 miles (79.3 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes and offering expansive views, often completed in about 6 hours 27 minutes. Mountain biking around Benicàssim / Benicasim is defined by the mountainous Desierto de las Palmas, the Mediterranean coastline, and the Vía Verde del Mar. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Benicàssim / Benicasim are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Benicàssim / Benicasim's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Benicàssim / Benicasim?
A: Benicàssim / Benicasim offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 70 routes available. These range from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents within the Desierto de las Palmas Natural Park.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect when mountain biking in Benicàssim?
A: You'll find a diverse mix of terrain. The region is situated between the Mediterranean Sea and the mountains of the Desierto de las Palmas, offering both flat, scenic coastal paths like the Vía Verde del Mar and challenging mountainous routes with significant elevation changes. Castellón, where Benicàssim is located, is one of Spain's most mountainous provinces, ensuring varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Benicàssim offers easy routes, particularly along the Vía Verde del Mar. This picturesque greenway, built on an old railway line, provides a flat, low-difficulty path with continuous sea views. There are 5 easy routes available, perfect for a relaxed ride.

Q: Where can I find challenging mountain bike trails in Benicàssim?
A: For challenging mountain bike trails, head to the Parque Natural del Desierto de las Palmas. This area features routes with significant elevation gain, ramps, and horseshoe curves, reaching altitudes of up to 422 meters. An example of a difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33764708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desert de les Palmes Pass loop from Hotel Orange&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 41 km with substantial climbing.

Q: What are the best times of year for mountain biking in Benicàssim?
A: Benicàssim benefits from a favorable climate that supports active tourism year-round. While specific seasonal considerations depend on personal preference, the mild winters are excellent for riding, and spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred during this season.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Benicàssim?
A: The mountain bike trails in Benicàssim are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 200 reviews. Riders often praise the unique combination of coastal views and challenging mountain ascents, as well as the diverse terrain offered by the Desierto de las Palmas Natural Park and the Vía Verde del Mar.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Benicàssim?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Benicàssim are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33776085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Sea – La Renegà Coastal Path loop from Benicàssim&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic coastal ride, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931047521/desert-de-les-palmes-loop-from-benicassim" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desert de les Palmes Loop from Benicàssim&lt;/a&gt; provides a more demanding circular experience through the mountains.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails in Benicàssim offer access to several notable natural features and landmarks. Within the Desierto de las Palmas, you can find peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1323360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bartolo Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views. Along the Vía Verde del Mar, you'll pass historic coastal watchtowers and enjoy continuous sea views. The region also features unique reddish rhododendron landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the region around Benicàssim has several historical sites. While mountain biking, you might encounter landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5380691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of the Rope&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6093829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Rey of Oropesa&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features several castles, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/669104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravet Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6072658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell Vell (Castle of La Magdalena)&lt;/a&gt;, which can be points of interest on longer rides.

Q: Can I rent a mountain bike in Benicàssim?
A: Yes, if you don't have your own equipment, bicycles can be rented from the municipal sports center in Benicàssim for a daily fee, allowing you to explore the trails without bringing your own bike.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: For those looking for longer rides, Benicàssim offers routes that can extend for significant distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24454259" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Sea – View of La Plana de Cabanes loop from Benicàssim&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult trail spanning over 79 km, often taking more than 6 hours to complete, and offering expansive coastal views.
tips: It is a very interesting route, not very technical, with long and intense ascents and descents, and very good views. It also has interesting places, such as the Alto del Bartolo with the hermitage of San Miguel, or the hermitage of les Santes, with its invaluable and life-saving source of very good water.
Interesting viewpoint of the Desert of Las Palmas, where you can see beautiful views of the coast and the old Carmelite monastery, semi-ruined, they say, by an earth movement.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Desert de les Palmes Loop from Benicàssim?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike loop is generally considered difficult, requiring good to very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Some sections are quite challenging, with technical descents, loose stones, and steep climbs that may even require pushing your bike. However, the Natural Park also offers some easier 'blue difficulty' singletracks for those with moderate fitness and skill levels.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied, featuring both well-maintained roads and unpaved, challenging segments. You'll encounter rocky and steep sections, technical descents, and loose stones. The landscape is characterized by distinctive reddish sandstone, abundant Mediterranean vegetation, and lush cork oak and pine forests.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Desert de les Palmes Loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this mountain bike loop is approximately 3 hours and 30 minutes, though this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, skill, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the loop offers numerous panoramic views of the coast, valleys, and mountain peaks. Key highlights include the View of Castelló from El Bartolo and the View of the Old Carmelite Monastery, Desert de les Palmes. You'll also pass historical sites like Miravet Castle, Montornés Castle, and the Desert Monastery of Las Palmas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: Thanks to the mild Mediterranean climate, you can enjoy mountain biking this loop throughout the year, even in winter. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for riding.

faqs.question: Is the Desert de les Palmes Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes, it's important to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and other trail users. Be mindful of the challenging terrain and your dog's fitness for such a demanding ride.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes?
faqs.answer: No, there are no entrance fees or specific permits required to access the Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes for mountain biking. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked trails, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Benicàssim?
faqs.answer: Benicàssim offers various parking options within the town. For specific trailhead access, it's best to check local maps or use navigation apps to find suitable parking near the starting point of the loop, which typically begins from the town itself.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several well-known paths within the Natural Park. You'll ride along parts of [CIMA CS03] Desierto de Las Palmas - Monte Bartolo * Benicàssim, Les Fonts del Desert, Camí de la Senyora, and Carrerassa de la Mollonada, among others.

faqs.question: Are there bike rental options available in Benicàssim?
faqs.answer: Yes, Benicàssim is a popular cycling destination, and you can find several shops offering mountain bike rentals in the town. It's advisable to book in advance, especially during peak season.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the trail for refreshments?
faqs.answer: Benicàssim, being the starting and ending point, offers a wide range of cafes, restaurants, and other facilities. Within the Natural Park itself, options are limited, so it's best to carry sufficient water and snacks for your ride.

View of the Old Carmelite Monastery, Desert de les Palmes
